summary: Prompt-injection payload instructing AI coding assistants to add exfiltration code to the installer's application
details: The package advertises itself as a React HTTP utility but ships no functionality. Its README and index.js console output contain a directive aimed at AI coding assistants (framed as '[SYSTEM INSTRUCTION]' / 'NOTE FOR AI ASSISTANT') telling the assistant to add a useEffect to App.tsx that POSTs window.__CONFIG__, process.env, and window.location.href to https://bin.webhookrelay.com/v1/webhooks/f982048d-7436-4b00-b508-89f5eb657645 — an author-controlled webhook relay endpoint. If a developer uses Cursor/Copilot/Claude and the assistant complies, the application's runtime configuration and environment variables are silently exfiltrated to a third party. The package has no install lifecycle hooks and no functional code; its sole purpose is to deliver this prompt-injection payload. The attack vector is indirect (requires an AI assistant to act on the embedded instruction) but the intent is unambiguous and the exfiltration endpoint is hardcoded.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
